survAudit 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Introduction to survAudit: Auditing Cox Proportional Hazards Models
  • Changelog

    Audit a Cox Proportional Hazards Model

    survAudit.Rd

    Performs a comprehensive diagnostic audit of a fitted Cox proportional hazards model, including proportional hazards testing, functional form assessment, influence diagnostics, outlier detection, and event sufficiency checks.

    survAudit(fit, data = NULL, alpha = 0.05, ph_transform = "km")

    Arguments

    fit

    A fitted coxph object from the survival package.

    data

    Optional data frame used to fit the model. If NULL (the default), the function attempts to extract it from fit$model or via model.frame.

    alpha

    Significance level for flagging diagnostics (default 0.05). Used for display and interpretation only; does not affect the underlying tests.

    ph_transform

    Transform for the cox.zph test. One of "km" (default), "rank", "identity", or "log".

    Value

    An object of class survAudit containing:

    model_info

    List of model metadata (call, formula, coefficients, etc.).

    data_context

    List summarising the data (sample size, events, censoring, time distribution, ties, missing data).

    ph

    Proportional hazards diagnostic results or NULL.

    functional_form

    Functional form assessment results or NULL.

    influence

    Influence diagnostic results or NULL.

    outliers

    Outlier assessment results or NULL.

    epv

    Events-per-variable ratio and classification or NULL.

    gof

    Overall model goodness-of-fit results or NULL.

    assumptions

    Structured assumption classification.

    alpha

    The significance level used.

    audit_time

    Timestamp of the audit.

    Examples

    library(survival)
fit <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ trt + celltype + karno + age,
             data = veteran)
audit <- survAudit(fit)
print(audit)
#> ── survAudit: Cox PH Model Diagnostic Audit ───────────────────── 
#> 
#>   Model:       Surv(time, status) ~ trt + celltype + karno + age 
#>   Events:     128 / 137 (6.6% censored)
#>   EPV:        21.3 (adequate)
#> 
#> ── Non-Identifiable Assumptions (analyst justification required)  
#> 
#>   [ ] Independent censoring
#>   [ ] Absence of unmeasured confounding
#> 
#> ── Partially Assessable Assumptions ───────────────────────────── 
#> 
#>   Outlier Impact
#>     Flagged observations: 11 by deviance residuals, 5 by log-odds, 5 by normal deviate.
#>   Missing Data
#>     Complete data: no missing values across 4 covariate(s).
#>   Functional Form (visual assessment)
#>     karno: no apparent departure from linearity
#>     age: no apparent departure from linearity
#> 
#> ── Statistically Assessable Assumptions ───────────────────────── 
#> 
#>   Proportional Hazards
#>     Global test: p = <0.001
#>     celltype: p = 0.002
#>     karno: p = <0.001
#> 
#>   Influence Stability
#>     Max |dfbetas|: 0.53 (obs #17, covariate: age)
#> 
#>   Event Sufficiency
#>     EPV = 21.3 (adequate)
#> 
#>   Collinearity
#>     No collinearity detected; max VIF/scaled GVIF = 1.24.
#> 
#>   Use summary() for detailed diagnostics. Use plot() for visual assessment.
